summary: Addressed security review feedback by removing raw provider HTTP error-body exposure from OpenAICompatibleClient and adding a regression test proving fake provider secrets/request material are not returned in structured HTTP errors.

tests_run.result: Passed: 11 tests across 4 suites, including the new non-OK fake-fetch provider body non-exposure regression.
Passed: Next.js production build completed. Build emitted the existing @next/swc-win32-x64-msvc invalid Win32 application warnings but exited successfully.

security_fix.finding: OpenAICompatibleClient HTTP error path returned raw provider error body in LlmCompletionError.details.
security_fix.resolution: HTTP errors now return only predictable metadata: code http_error, generic message, status, and details.providerError=true. The raw response body is not read or exposed.
security_fix.regression_test: tests/llm-json-client.test.ts verifies a 401 fake provider body containing sk-test-secret-do-not-leak and request material is absent from the serialized structured error.

decisions: Did not attempt redaction of provider bodies; removed body exposure entirely to avoid partial sanitization risk.
Kept status code available for caller handling while making details deterministic and non-sensitive.
risks: Provider-specific HTTP error diagnostics are intentionally no longer surfaced through the client result; future debugging should use controlled server-side logging with explicit redaction if needed.
Next build still reports the pre-existing SWC Windows binary warning despite successful exit.
requested_action: Re-review the HTTP error handling path and regression test for acceptance.
